text:
(In the case of an application commenced elsewhere than in the Family Court)

This application was transferred to this court from the [specify] [Division] / [Court] by order of [name of judge] on [date].

The arbitration has commenced and is continuing.

The arbitrator's order is (so far as relevant to this application) in the following terms: [set out (in the words of the arbitrator's order)]

This application is for an order under section 42 of the Arbitration Act 1996 for the enforcement of the arbitrator's order and is made [by the arbitrator, upon notice to the parties] / [by the applicant with the permission of the tribunal, upon notice to the other party/ies)] / [by the applicant, the parties having agreed that the powers of the court under this section shall be available].

This court is satisfied that:

the applicant has exhausted all available arbitral process in respect of the respondent's failure to comply with [that] / [those] provision[s] of the peremptory order; and

the respondent has failed to comply with [that] / [those] provision[s] of the peremptory order [within the time prescribed by the order] / [within a reasonable time, no time for compliance having been prescribed].

Agreements

[Set out]

Undertakings to the court

[Set out]

IT IS ORDERED [BY CONSENT]:

(either)

The respondent shall comply with the peremptory order by no later than [date].

(or)

[Insert other orders]

(If applied for) Permission to appeal against this decision is [granted] / [refused].

[Insert provision for costs]

Dated  [date]

(Where undertakings have been given)

You may be held to be in contempt of court and imprisoned or fined, or your assets may be seized, if you break the promises that you have given to the court.

If you fail to pay any sum of money which you have promised the court that you will pay, a person entitled to enforce the undertaking may apply to the court for an order. You may be sent to prison if it is proved that you-

a. have, or have had since the date of your undertaking, the means to pay the sum; and

b. have refused or neglected, or are refusing or neglecting, to pay that sum.
